articles.title: SNAPCHAT'S NEW LENS LETS YOU CREATE AI IMAGES USING TEXT PROMPTS
articles.summary: Snapchat's Imagine Lens allows Snapchat+ Platinum and Lens+ subscribers to create and edit images using text prompts like "turn me into an alien." The open prompt image-generation Lens features both custom prompts and pre-loaded options for comic panels or caricatures. It costs $15.99 per month for Platinum or $8.99 for Lens+ subscriptions. The release demonstrates Snap's continued AI investment alongside its AR leadership.

articles.title: SCENE IT TO BELIEVE IT: POPULATE 3D WORLDS QUICKLY WITH NVIDIA AI BLUEPRINTS
articles.summary: NVIDIA released an AI Blueprint for 3D object generation that enables artists to create up to 20 3D objects from text prompts, streamlining the traditionally time-consuming prototyping process. The workflow integrates Microsoft's TRELLIS model via a new NVIDIA NIM microservice, which generates high-quality 3D assets 20% faster than the native model. Artists can automatically export generated objects to Blender or other 3D applications. The system works on NVIDIA GeForce RTX GPUs with 16GB or more memory.

articles.title: ADOBE'S LATEST LEGAL CLASH DOESN'T COME AS A SURPRISE
articles.summary: Adobe is facing a new class action lawsuit accusing it of misleading customers with unclear subscription terms, hidden fees, and intentionally difficult cancellation processes that leave users feeling trapped. The case reflects growing backlash over Adobe's pricing, AI changes, and business practices, threatening the company's reputation among creatives.

articles.title: A PRACTICAL GUIDE TO HARNESSING AI FOR DESIGN WORK
articles.summary: Generative AI offers rapid creative output, but designers must resist treating initial AI results as finished work and instead focus on disciplined iteration and craft. The "EDEN Loop" framework (Explore, Diagnose, Elevate, and Nurture) helps designers harness AI as a creative multiplier rather than replacement, emphasizing the need for human judgment in art direction and brand storytelling. The approach cautions against allowing emerging designers to master AI prompts before they have learned fundamental design principles, instead advocating for AI as an amplification tool that still requires human pilots to navigate toward authentic, culturally sensitive brand work.

articles.title: HOW TO MAKE A BIG IMPACT WITH A SMALL TEAM: DESIGN OPS AT SLACK
articles.summary: Slack's Design Operations team demonstrates how a small three-person team can drive significant impact by strategically dividing responsibilities across business strategy, people programs, and product systems. The team operates through focused leadership alignment, thoughtful designer experience programs, and scalable processes that provide structure while maintaining flexibility. Their collaborative approach emphasizes working smarter rather than larger, proving that lean teams can create meaningful organizational change when they prioritize strategic focus and cross-functional partnership.

articles.title: HOW PALS' EUROVISION REFRESH POINTS TOWARDS A DIFFERENT DESIGN STUDIO MODEL
articles.summary: Eurovision commissioned Sheffield-based agency PALS to refresh its brand ahead of the contest's 70th anniversary, embracing the studio's freelance “hub and spoke” model to create a flexible, digitally adaptable identity with a new beating-heart logo and custom typeface. The project highlights a shift away from traditional agencies toward diverse, freelance-driven teams, though fan reactions to the new look have been mixed.

articles.title: CREATE CAPTIVATING SLIDES EFFORTLESSLY
articles.summary: Transform your ideas and documents into captivating slides with AI.

articles.title: AI WEBSITE BUILDER
articles.summary: Start, iterate, and launch a website all in one place - no code, no drag-and-drop, just prompt.

articles.title: KELP UI
articles.summary: Kelp is a UI library for people who love HTML powered by modern CSS and Web Components.

articles.title: IDC STUDY SAYS THE GLOBAL WORKFORCE ENGAGED IN SOFTWARE DESIGN IS EXPANDING
articles.summary: IDC research forecasts the global software design workforce will expand by over 30% from 107 million to 144 million people between 2025 and 2029. UX design roles are expected to grow fastest at 7.6% annually, outpacing other knowledge worker categories due to increasing demand for design-intensive solutions. Generative AI will accelerate product development while creating pressure for designers to deliver highly usable and visually appealing AI features.

articles.title: THE ICEBERG UX MODEL: DESIGNING FOR AI-FIRST INTERFACES
articles.summary: The Iceberg UX Model emphasizes that 90% of an AI product's user experience lies beneath the visible interface, focusing on error handling, trust mechanisms, and failure recovery rather than just polished UI design. Most AI product teams fail by over-prioritizing visual design while neglecting invisible layers like fallback flows, confidence indicators, and system transparency that determine user trust and retention. A successful AI-first UX workflow should start with user needs, map failure states, test trust patterns, and build foundational invisible layers before designing the interface.

articles.title: BRUTAL TYPES: UNEARTHING EUROPE'S FORGOTTEN INTERWAR LETTERFORMS
articles.summary: Paula Mastrangelo and Ramiro Espinoza launched “Brutal Types” as a separate foundry from Retype to focus on expressive display fonts inspired by Europe's interwar lettering, especially the Dutch avant-garde. Drawing on fieldwork, archives, and overlooked sources, they and their collaborators reinterpret historic alphabets into contemporary typefaces, offering designers distinctive alternatives for branding and media while planning future releases and a book on monumental Dutch lettering.

articles.title: YOU DON'T NEED ANIMATIONS
articles.summary: Effective animations serve clear purposes like explaining features, providing feedback, or creating spatial consistency, but they can harm user experience when overused or poorly implemented.

articles.title: EDIE MEDLEY ILLUSTRATES THE CONVERSATIONS SHE OVERHEARS WHILE WORKING IN A PUB
articles.summary: Illustrator Edie Medley transforms overheard pub conversations into playful drawings, capturing both the humor and intimacy of everyday social life while celebrating pubs as spaces of community.
